$comment: Reference TEMPLATE. By itself it does nothing -- scripts/*/register-claude-code-mcp.*
merges the entries you name into ~/.claude.json (USER scope).
DO NOT register `graphify` or `graphify-docker` from here into user scope (2026-07-19).
Both are repo-bound: they resolve graphify-out/graph.json relative to a single mounted
path, so one user-scope entry serves ONE repo's graph to EVERY repo. That was the actual
bug -- a user-scope graphify-docker pinned to -w /home/s/code/agent-skills meant Invest
and Server silently queried agent-skills' graph. It has been removed from ~/.claude.json.
Graphify belongs in PROJECT scope: give each repo a .mcp.json with its own
.mcp.json). Do not rely on a project entry to out-rank a user-scope one: the published
order is local > project > user, but this repo has a 2026-07-17 incident on record where
a user-scope `omnigraph` won anyway (an unapproved .mcp.json loads no project servers at
all, which looks identical). Keep user scope EMPTY of both instead -- then there is
nothing to lose to, whichever rule applies. See CLAUDE.md 'MCP config precedence'.
Same applies to `omnigraph` (pinned per repo via OMNIGRAPH_GRAPH_ID). Serena and
playwright are genuinely repo-agnostic and are fine in user scope.
